scope: Windmill — developer platform: scripts, flows, schedules, apps

personas.name: Developer
personas.description: A new developer signing in to Windmill for the first time to write and schedule a small script.

personas.name: Admin
personas.description: Workspace admin: can manage users, groups, settings within a workspace.

personas.name: Operator
personas.description: Read-only / run-only workspace member: can execute scripts/flows/apps but not create or edit.

personas.name: Superadmin
personas.description: Instance-level superadmin: manages global users, instance groups, instance settings.

nodes.title: Script detail with auto-generated run UI
Flows list (workspace Flows tab on Home)
Flow detail with auto-generated run UI + graph viewer

edges.label: Save → modal closes; row visible at /schedules
Click row OR 'New folder' → opens Folder drawer in place
Direct nav to /global_drafts (dev-only; requires localStorage.wm_dev_global_ai='1'; no sidebar entry)
Apps toggle on Home → row click → /apps/get/<path>

features.name: Workspaces
features.summary: Workspaces are the unit of multi-tenancy in Windmill: each user belongs to one or more workspaces (each with its own scripts, flows, schedules, users, and settings), and every (logged) feature route is scoped to the workspace currently held in localStorage. A fresh user is routed to /user/workspaces until one is selected or created; non-admin users on Community Edition are capped at 2 workspaces beyond the default 'admins'; settings edits require workspace-admin.

flows.title: Create a new workspace from the picker and land on Home
flows.story: As a new developer with no workspace yet, I click '+ Create a new workspace' on /user/workspaces, submit a workspace name and ID, and land on the workspace home so I can use feature routes.

steps.action: On /user/workspaces (title 'Workspace Selection | Windmill', h1 'Select a workspace'), click the link '+ Create a new workspace'
On /user/create_workspace (h1 'New Workspace'), fill 'Workspace name' with 'Acme'
Fill 'Workspace ID' with a slug matching ^\w+(-\w+)*$ (e.g. 'acme-<rand>')
Click the button 'Create workspace'
Land on / (Workspace home) — localStorage.workspace is now set to the chosen ID

scenarios.statement:
Given I am a signed-in developer with no workspace selected
And I am on /user/workspaces
When I click the '+ Create a new workspace' link
And I fill 'Workspace name' with 'Acme'
And I fill 'Workspace ID' with 'acme-<rand>'
And I click 'Create workspace'
Then I land on /
And localStorage.workspace equals 'acme-<rand>'

Given I am on /user/create_workspace
When I fill 'Workspace ID' with 'Invalid ID!'
Then the form rejects the value and the 'Create workspace' button is disabled

Given I am a non-superadmin on Community Edition
And I already own 2 workspaces outside of 'admins'
When I POST /api/workspaces/create with a new workspace
Then the request is rejected with HTTP 400
And the error reads 'You have reached the maximum number of workspaces (2 outside of default workspace \'admins\') without an enterprise license.'

flows.title: Pick an existing workspace from the picker
flows.story: As a developer with one or more existing workspaces, I open /user/workspaces and click a workspace tile to select it, landing on Home with that workspace as the active one.

steps.action:
On /user/workspaces (h1 'Select a workspace'), under the h2 'Workspaces' section, see one button per workspace I belong to (accessible name pattern e.g. 'Admins
-
admins
as superadmin …')
Click the workspace tile button for the workspace I want
Land on / — localStorage.workspace is set to that workspace's slug

scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in and I belong to workspace 'admins'
And I am on /user/workspaces
When I click the workspace tile labeled 'Admins - admins as superadmin'
Then I land on /
And localStorage.workspace equals 'admins'

flows.title: Accessing a (logged) feature route without localStorage.workspace redirects to the picker
flows.story: As a developer whose browser has no localStorage.workspace yet (fresh sign-in or cleared storage), I navigate to any (logged) feature route and am redirected to /user/workspaces to select or create one first.

steps.action: Clear localStorage.workspace (and sessionStorage.workspace)
Navigate to any (logged) feature route (e.g. /scripts/add, /, /runs, /schedules, /workspace_settings)
Be redirected to /user/workspaces?rd=<URL-encoded-intended-route> (h1 'Select a workspace')

scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in
And localStorage.workspace is empty
When I navigate to /scripts/add
Then I land on /user/workspaces?rd=/scripts/add

flows.title: Edit workspace name and color from Workspace Settings
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open /workspace_settings → General, change the workspace name and color, and see the new values reflected in the workspace selector and sidebar.

steps.action: From Home, navigate to /workspace_settings?tab=general (sidebar Settings → Workspace, or direct URL)
Confirm h1 'Workspace settings: <workspace-id>' and h2 'General'
Change the 'Workspace name' field (e.g. to 'Acme Renamed')
Optionally change 'Workspace color' / 'Workspace ID'
Save — the change is recorded server-side (audit_log kind=Update), and the workspace selector reflects the new name and color

scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in as a non-admin member of workspace 'acme'
When I navigate to /workspace_settings
Then the 'Workspace name' input is not editable (admin-only sections are hidden)

Given I am a workspace admin on /workspace_settings
When I change the workspace name to 'Acme Renamed' and Save
Then POST /api/w/<id>/workspaces/change_workspace_name succeeds with 200
And an audit log entry of kind Update is recorded

flows.title: Add a user to the workspace (admin)
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open Workspace Settings → Users, click 'Add new user', enter an email and pick a role, and the new member or pending invite is visible on the Users tab.

steps.action: On /workspace_settings?tab=users (default landing tab; h2 'Members (<n>)' + 'Operator settings')
Click the 'Add new user' button
Enter the user's email and pick a role (operator / developer / admin)
Submit the dialog — the action either adds the user immediately or sends an invite, depending on whether the email belongs to an existing Windmill user
On success the new row references the email in the page (member row or 'Invites' section)

scenarios.statement:
Given I am a non-admin operator in workspace 'acme'
When I POST /api/w/acme/workspaces/invite_user
Then the response is HTTP 403

Given Windmill is running on Community Edition
And I am a superadmin
When I POST /api/w/admins/workspaces/invite_user with a non-admin email
Then the response is HTTP 400 with an EE-only error message

flows.title: Leave a workspace (non-admin member)
flows.story: As a NON-ADMIN member of a workspace I no longer need, I leave it from Workspace Settings; admin owners cannot leave their own workspace and must archive or delete it instead.

steps.action: Precondition: I am a non-admin member of the workspace (not the owner / admin)
From /workspace_settings, click the 'Leave workspace' button (only rendered for non-admin members)
Confirm the destructive action
POST /api/w/<id>/workspaces/leave returns 200; land on /user/workspaces; the workspace is no longer in my list

scenarios.statement:
Given I am a NON-ADMIN member of workspace 'acme'
When I POST /api/w/acme/workspaces/leave
Then the response is HTTP 200
And my user row in workspace 'acme' is removed

flows.title: Archive a workspace (admin)
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open /workspace_settings → Advanced and archive the workspace; subsequent (logged) routes for that workspace become inaccessible until a superadmin unarchives it.

steps.action: On /workspace_settings?tab=advanced, click 'Archive workspace'
Confirm the destructive action — POST /api/w/<id>/workspaces/archive returns 200
Land on /user/workspaces — the workspace is hidden from non-superadmin pickers; superadmin can unarchive via POST /workspaces/unarchive/<id>

scenarios.statement:
Given I am a non-admin member of workspace 'acme'
When I POST /api/w/acme/workspaces/archive
Then the response is HTTP 403

Given workspace 'acme' is archived
And I am a superadmin
When I POST /workspaces/unarchive/acme
Then the response is HTTP 200
And 'acme' reappears in the workspace picker for its members

features.name: Scripts (Python/TS/Go/Bash editor & runs)
features.summary: Author a script in the Monaco editor, pick a language, and deploy it to a workspace path; deploy lands on the script detail with an auto-generated run UI.

flows.title: Author and deploy a Python script from the workspace home
flows.story: As a developer in a fresh workspace, I open the Script editor from Home, pick Python, replace the body with a no-arg hello-windmill script, and Deploy — landing on the script detail page with its auto-generated run UI.

steps.action: From Home, click the 'Script' CTA — /scripts/add redirects to /scripts/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>
In the Language picker, click 'Python'
Replace the editor body with `def main():\n    return 'hello windmill'` and wait for 'Saved'
Click the 'Deploy' button — land on /scripts/get/<scriptPath>

flows.title: Author and deploy a TypeScript (bun) script from Home
flows.story: As a developer, I open the Script editor from Home and pick the TypeScript (bun) language; the editor body switches to the bun template, and after replacing it with a no-arg hello-windmill script I Deploy and land on the script detail page with its auto-generated run UI.

steps.action: From Home, click the 'Script' CTA — lands on /scripts/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid> with Python selected by default
In the Language picker, click the 'TypeScript (Bun)' tile — the editor body is replaced with the bun template
Replace the editor body with `export async function main() { return 'hello windmill' }` and wait for the 'Autosave status' to read 'Saved'
Click the 'Deploy' button — land on /scripts/get/<scriptPath> with the auto-generated run form visible

scenarios.statement:
Given I am a developer on the Script editor from Home
When I click the 'TypeScript (Bun)' tile in the Language picker
And I replace the editor body with a no-arg hello-windmill bun script
And I click the 'Deploy' button
Then I land on the /scripts/get/<path> detail page
And the auto-generated run form is visible

flows.title: Edit an existing script's body and re-deploy to create a new version
flows.story: As a developer, I open a previously deployed script's detail page, click Edit to land on the editor at its workspace path, modify the body, click Deploy again, and the script detail page reflects the new content with a new hash recorded in version history.

steps.action: On the script detail page (/scripts/get/<path>), click the 'Edit' button — navigate to /scripts/edit/<path>
Modify the editor body (e.g. change the return string) and wait for 'Saved'
Click 'Deploy' — a confirmation modal may appear if other deployers have edits to merge
Land back on /scripts/get/<path> with the new body in effect; a new hash entry is recorded in the version history

scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed script at 'u/admin/scripts-s03-<rand>' returning 'v1'
When I open its detail page and click Edit
And I change the body to return 'v2'
And I click Deploy
Then I land on the script detail page for that path
And the editor of that script shows the new body returning 'v2'

features.name: Flows (multi-step workflows)
features.summary: Flows are multi-step workflows that chain script steps, sub-flows, for/while loops, branches, approval/prompt steps, and AI agents in a visual graph. Authored in FlowBuilder (a drag-and-drop builder at /flows/add and /flows/edit/<path>) and persisted via FlowService.createFlow / updateFlow as a workspace-pathed record (POST /api/w/<id>/flows/create, /update/<path>). Deployed flows are listed on the Home Flows sub-tab (Windmill has no standalone /flows route), and each has its own detail page at /flows/get/<path> with an auto-generated RunForm built from the flow's input schema and a FlowGraphViewer rendering of its modules. Triggering a deployed flow POSTs /api/w/<id>/jobs/run/f/<path> and navigates to /run/<jobId>, where FlowProgressBar + FlowStatusViewer surface per-step status as the job executes. Operators cannot create flows (server-side 403); creates on Cloud are capped at 1000 per workspace.

flows.title: Author and deploy a 2-step Python flow from Home
flows.story: As a developer in a fresh workspace, I open the Flow editor from Home, add a first Python step that returns a value, add a second Python step that echoes its parent step's result back, and click Deploy — landing on /flows/get/<flowPath> with the auto-generated RunForm and the graph viewer of my two-step flow.

steps.action: From Home, click the 'Flow' CTA (CreateActionsFlow) — the 'Create a new flow' modal opens with two tiles: 'Flow Editor' and 'Workflow-as-Code'
Click the 'Flow Editor' tile — navigate to /flows/add, which redirects via makeDraftAddLoad to /flows/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>?new_draft=true
From the FlowBuilder graph's '+' inserter, pick 'Action' → add an inline Python script step (step 1) whose body is `def main():\n    return 'hello'` and wait for autosave
Click the next '+' inserter and add a second inline Python step (step 2) whose body is `def main(prev):\n    return prev` with input_transform on `prev` referencing step 1's result
Click the 'Deploy' button (FlowBuilder → DeployButton) — FlowService.createFlow is called against /api/w/<w_id>/flows/create
Land on /flows/get/<flowPath> with the auto-generated RunForm and a FlowGraphViewer showing two ordered step nodes

scenarios.statement:
Given I am a developer on Home in a fresh workspace
When I click the 'Flow' CTA and pick 'Flow Editor' in the modal
And I land on /flows/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>?new_draft=true
And I add a first inline Python step returning 'hello'
And I add a second inline Python step echoing step 1's result
And I click the 'Deploy' button
Then FlowService.createFlow is called (POST /api/w/<w_id>/flows/create) and returns 201
And I land on /flows/get/<flowPath>
And the FlowGraphViewer renders two ordered step nodes

Given I am signed in as a workspace operator
When I POST /api/w/<w_id>/flows/create with a valid NewFlow payload
Then the response is HTTP 403 with message 'Operators cannot create flows for security reasons'

flows.title: Run a deployed flow from its detail page
flows.story: As a developer on a deployed flow's detail page, I submit the auto-generated RunForm; the JobService.runFlowByPath call goto's /run/<jobId>, where the FlowProgressBar and FlowStatusViewer render the run completing with a Success status.

steps.action: On /flows/get/<flowPath>, fill the auto-generated RunForm (no args for a no-arg flow) and click the 'Run' button
The page calls JobService.runFlowByPath against POST /api/w/<w_id>/jobs/run/f/<flowPath> and goto's /run/<jobId>
On /run/<jobId>, verify the h1 'run/<jobId>' is visible
Verify the FlowProgressBar renders (job_kind === 'flow' branch) and the FlowStatusViewer shows per-step completion
Verify the job-status badge renders as the green Check (Success) once both steps complete

scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed flow at <flowPath>
And I am on /flows/get/<flowPath>
When I click the 'Run' button on the auto-generated RunForm
Then POST /api/w/<w_id>/jobs/run/f/<flowPath> returns 201 with a jobId
And I land on /run/<jobId>
And the FlowProgressBar renders the flow's modules
And the job-status badge ends as the green Check (Success)

flows.title: Edit an existing flow and re-deploy with a new step appended
flows.story: As a developer iterating on a deployed flow, I open the flow detail page, click 'Edit' to land on /flows/edit/<flowPath>, append a third inline Python step from the InsertModule popover, click Deploy, and the detail page reflects the new 3-step graph.

steps.action: On /flows/get/<flowPath> for a 2-step flow, click the 'Edit' button (DetailPageHeader → button labelled 'Edit') — navigate to /flows/edit/<flowPath>
From the trailing '+' inserter on the graph, pick 'Action' → add a third inline Python step
Wait for autosave, then click the 'Deploy' button — FlowService.updateFlow is called against /api/w/<w_id>/flows/update/<path>
Land back on /flows/get/<flowPath> with a 3-step FlowGraphViewer; a new entry is recorded in the flow's version history

scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed 2-step flow at <flowPath>
When I open /flows/get/<flowPath> and click the 'Edit' button
And I land on /flows/edit/<flowPath>
And I add a third inline Python step from the InsertModule popover
And I click 'Deploy'
Then POST /api/w/<w_id>/flows/update/<flowPath> returns 200
And I land on /flows/get/<flowPath>
And the FlowGraphViewer now renders 3 ordered step nodes

flows.title: Delete a flow from its detail page menu
flows.story: As a developer cleaning up an unused flow, I open /flows/get/<flowPath>, open the actions menu, click 'Delete', and the flow is removed from the workspace (DELETE /api/w/<id>/flows/delete/<path>); subsequent GETs at the path 404.

steps.action: On /flows/get/<flowPath>, open the actions menu in the DetailPageHeader
Click the 'Delete' menu item (red, Trash icon) — FlowService.deleteFlowByPath is called against DELETE /api/w/<w_id>/flows/delete/<flowPath>
A 'Flow deleted' toast appears; the page goto's '/' (Home)
Verify the Flows sub-tab on Home (/?kind=flow#workspace) no longer lists <flowPath>

scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed flow at <flowPath>
And I am on /flows/get/<flowPath>
When I open the actions menu and click 'Delete'
Then DELETE /api/w/<w_id>/flows/delete/<flowPath> returns 200
And I land on '/'
And the Home Flows sub-tab no longer lists <flowPath>

flows.title: View a flow run's per-step status in the graph
flows.story: As a developer reviewing a flow run, I open /run/<jobId> for a completed flow job; the FlowStatusViewer renders the flow's sub-graph with per-module status (Success / Failure / Running) so I can see which step finished or failed.

steps.action: Trigger a flow run from /flows/get/<flowPath> — land on /run/<jobId>
Verify the FlowProgressBar renders (job_kind === 'flow' branch in run/+page.svelte)
Verify the FlowStatusViewer renders below, showing one status node per module in job.flow_status.modules
Verify each module node's status icon corresponds to JobStatusIcon's enum (green Check = Success, red X = Failure, yellow Play = Running, gray Ban = Canceled)

scenarios.statement:
Given I just ran a 2-step flow at <flowPath> successfully
And I am on /run/<jobId> for that flow job
Then the FlowProgressBar is visible
And the FlowStatusViewer renders one node per module in job.flow_status.modules
And every module node's status icon is the green Check (Success)

features.name: Runs and job history
features.summary: Trigger a deployed script from its auto-generated Run form, watch the job land on the /run/<jobId> detail page, and discover/manage past jobs in the /runs history. The detail page renders the run status as a colored icon badge (green Check = Success, red X = Failure, gray Ban = Canceled, yellow Play = Running, violet/blue Hourglass = Suspended/Queued), shows the script's return value in a 'Result' panel, and exposes Cancel + 'Run again' actions. /runs is a filterable list (status / path / user / folder / tag) with columns Duration, Path, Triggered by, and (when wide enough) Tag.

flows.title: Run a deployed script from its auto-generated UI and see Success
flows.story: As a developer on a freshly deployed script, I submit the auto-generated Run form and land on the job-detail page, where the run renders as a green Check status badge and the 'Result' panel contains my script's return value.

steps.action: On /scripts/get/<scriptPath>, fill the auto-generated RunForm and click the 'Run' button
The page calls JobService.runScriptByHash (or runScriptByPath for hub scripts) and goto's /run/<jobId>
On /run/<jobId>, verify the h1 'run/<jobId>' is visible, the status renders as the green Check badge (Success), and the 'Result' panel contains the script's return value ('hello windmill')

scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed script at <scriptPath> that returns 'hello windmill'
And I am on /scripts/get/<scriptPath>
When I click the 'Run' button on the auto-generated RunForm
Then I land on /run/<jobId>
And the h1 'run/<jobId>' is visible
And the job-status badge renders as the green Check (Success)
And the 'Result' panel contains 'hello windmill'

flows.title: Find a completed run in the runs history
flows.story: As a developer who just ran (or scheduled) a script, I navigate to /runs from the sidebar and see at least one successful row for my script in the runs history table (rendered as a green Check icon link, not a literal 'Success' label).

steps.action: Navigate to /runs from the sidebar
Verify the page h1 'Runs' is visible
Verify the runs table renders columns 'Duration', 'Path', 'Triggered by'
Verify at least one row whose 'Path' column references <scriptPath> is visible, with a green Check status badge

scenarios.statement:
Given I just ran script <scriptPath> successfully (jobId known)
When I navigate to /runs from the sidebar
Then the h1 'Runs' is visible
And the runs table renders columns 'Duration', 'Path', and 'Triggered by'
And a row whose Path column references <scriptPath> is visible
And that row's status badge is the green Check (Success)

flows.title: Filter runs history by status
flows.story: As a developer triaging recent activity, I open the Status filter on /runs and pick 'Success' (or 'Failure', 'Running'); the runs table narrows to only matching rows.

steps.action: Navigate to /runs from the sidebar
Open the 'Status' filter in the FilterSearchbar and choose 'Success'
Verify the URL is updated (the filter is URL-synced) and that all visible rows render the green Check badge
Switch the Status filter to 'Failure' — only rows with the red X badge remain (or the empty-state 'No jobs found for the selected filters.' appears if none exist)

scenarios.statement:
Given I am on /runs
And at least one successful run for <scriptPath> exists
When I open the 'Status' filter and pick 'Success'
Then every row visible in the runs table renders the green Check status badge
And a row referencing <scriptPath> is visible

Given the /runs page
When I open the 'Status' filter
Then the only available options are: All, Running, Success, Failure, Canceled, Waiting, Suspended

flows.title: Filter runs history by runnable path
flows.story: As a developer wanting to see only this script's runs, I either navigate to /runs/<scriptPath> directly or set the 'Path' filter on /runs; the table narrows to rows whose Path column matches that runnable.

steps.action: Navigate to /runs/<scriptPath> (the route's initialPath param pre-seeds the Path filter)
Verify the h1 'Runs' is visible and the 'Path' filter chip shows <scriptPath>
Verify every visible row's 'Path' column references <scriptPath>
Clear the Path filter — unrelated rows reappear

scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed script at <scriptPath> with at least one completed run
When I navigate to /runs/<scriptPath>
Then the h1 'Runs' is visible
And the Path filter is set to <scriptPath>
And every visible row's 'Path' column references <scriptPath>

flows.title: Cancel a running job from its detail page
flows.story: As a developer who launched a long-running script, I open /run/<jobId> while it is still running, click 'Cancel', and the job's status flips to the gray Ban (Canceled) badge.

steps.action: Launch a script that runs long enough to observe (e.g. one that sleeps a few seconds) from /scripts/get/<scriptPath> — land on /run/<jobId>
While the status badge is the yellow Play (Running), click the 'Cancel' button
The frontend POSTs /api/w/<w_id>/jobs_u/queue/cancel/<jobId>
After the cancel completes, the status badge flips to the gray Ban (Canceled)

scenarios.statement:
Given I am on /run/<jobId> and the job is still Running (yellow Play badge)
When I click the 'Cancel' button
Then POST /api/w/<w_id>/jobs_u/queue/cancel/<jobId> returns 200
And the status badge flips to the gray Ban (Canceled)

Given /run/<jobId> for a job whose state is 'CompletedJob'
When I view the action row
Then no 'Cancel' button is rendered

flows.title: Re-run a completed job from its detail page
flows.story: As a developer reviewing a completed run, I click 'Run again' on /run/<jobId> to re-execute the same runnable; the script-run page is reopened with the original args pre-filled, and an immediate 'Run immediately with same args' dropdown option queues a fresh job in one click.

steps.action: On /run/<jobId> for a completed script (job_kind == 'script' or 'flow'), locate the 'Run again' button
Click 'Run again' — the page goto's /scripts/get/<scriptHash>#<sharableArgsHash>, re-opening the auto-generated RunForm with the original args pre-filled
(Alternatively, open the 'Run again' dropdown and pick 'Run immediately with same args' to queue a new job without revisiting the form)
Submit the form — a new /run/<newJobId> page is loaded with its own status badge

scenarios.statement:
Given I am on /run/<jobId> for a completed script run of <scriptPath>
When I click the 'Run again' button
Then the page navigates to /scripts/get/<scriptHash>#<argsHash>
And the RunForm is pre-populated with the original args
When I click 'Run' on that form
Then I land on /run/<newJobId> for a new job whose script_path is <scriptPath>

flows.title: View a failed job and see the error in the Result panel
flows.story: As a developer debugging a broken script, I open /run/<jobId> for a failed run; the status renders as the red X badge and the 'Result' panel contains the error payload (DisplayResult renders the failure object).

steps.action: Run a script that throws an error (e.g. `throw new Error('boom')`) from /scripts/get/<scriptPath>
Land on /run/<jobId>
Verify the status badge is the red X (Failure)
Verify the 'Result' panel is visible and contains the error payload (the script's thrown message, as rendered by DisplayResult)

scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed script at <scriptPath> that throws 'boom'
When I run it from /scripts/get/<scriptPath>
And I land on /run/<jobId>
Then the status badge is the red X (Failure)
And the 'Result' panel is visible
And the rendered result contains 'boom'

features.name: Schedules (cron)
features.summary: Workspace schedules run a deployed script or flow on a cron expression. Schedules can be created from the Triggers panel of a script or from the standalone /schedules page; each schedule is workspace-scoped, has a unique path, a cron + timezone, an enabled toggle, optional pause-until, retry/error/recovery handlers, and produces queued jobs whose results show up in the runs history.

flows.title: Schedule a deployed script on a cron from its Triggers panel
flows.story: As a developer on a deployed script, I open Triggers → Add trigger → Schedule, set a cron in the schedule editor, and save — closing the editor with the schedule attached to the script.

steps.action: On /scripts/get/<scriptPath>, switch to the 'Triggers' tab in the script-detail panel
Click 'Add trigger' and pick 'Schedule' from the chooser
In the schedule editor, fill the Cron textbox with '* * * * *'
Click 'Save' — the schedule editor heading is hidden and the schedule is attached to the script

scenarios.statement:
Given I am on /scripts/get/<scriptPath> for a freshly deployed script
And the 'Triggers' tab is open
When I click 'Add trigger' and pick 'Schedule'
And I fill the Cron textbox with '* * * * *'
And I click 'Save'
Then the schedule editor closes
And a schedule with that cron is attached to <scriptPath>

Given I am on the schedule editor for a deployed script
When I fill the Cron textbox with 'not a cron'
And I click 'Save'
Then the save fails with a cron-validation error
And no schedule row is created for that path

flows.title: See a created schedule listed on /schedules
flows.story: As a developer who just created a schedule, I navigate to /schedules from the sidebar and see a row whose cron expression matches what I saved.

steps.action: Navigate to /schedules from the sidebar
Verify the Schedules heading is visible
Verify a row referencing the saved cron expression is visible

scenarios.statement:
Given I created a schedule at 'u/admin/sc02-<rand>' on cron '* * * * *' for script <scriptPath>
When I navigate to /schedules from the sidebar
Then the Schedules heading is visible

features.name: Folders
features.summary: Folders are workspace-scoped path namespaces under f/<name>/ that bundle scripts/flows/apps/schedules/variables/resources behind a shared ACL. The /folders page lists them, the FolderEditor drawer manages members (admin/writer/viewer) and optional default_permissioned_as overrides. Folder names match ^[a-zA-Z_0-9-]+$ (backend VALID_FOLDER_NAME). Membership is enforced by the backend: only owners ('admin' role) can add/remove other owners, and only owners can delete the folder; non-owners listing /folders/list will simply not see it (user_db RLS). Creating a folder via UI immediately opens its FolderEditor drawer for further setup.

flows.story: As a developer/admin, I open /folders, click 'New folder', type a name in the popover, hit 'Create', and the new folder appears as a row in the folders table (the editor drawer also opens in place for follow-up configuration).

Confirm title 'Folders | Windmill' and h1 'Folders'

Click the popover 'Create' button — POST /api/w/<id>/folders/create returns 200; the FolderEditor drawer opens with title 'Folder <name>'
Close the drawer — the new folder row is visible in the table (Name cell contains the folder name)

features.name: Triggers (HTTP routes & webhooks)
features.summary: Non-schedule entrypoints that invoke a deployed runnable. SCOPED to two trigger types that work out-of-the-box in the single-process Community Edition sandbox: (1) custom HTTP routes — managed at /routes, sidebar 'TRIGGERS' → 'HTTP'; admin-configurable path / method / target runnable; resolved at runtime by /api/r/<route_path> (or /api/r/<workspace_id>/<route_path> when workspaced_route is set or instance setting http_route_workspaced_route is true); (2) per-runnable webhooks — auto-generated on every deployed script/flow (no create UI), exposed via the Triggers tab on /scripts/get/:path and /flows/get/:path under a 'Webhooks' section that prints the canonical /api/w/<id>/jobs/run/p/<path> (async), /run_wait_result/p/<path> (sync), /run_and_stream/p/<path> (SSE) URLs. Out of scope on purpose: WebSockets, Postgres, Kafka, NATS, SQS, GCP Pub/Sub, Azure Event Grid, MQTT, Email — all of these require external infrastructure and most are Enterprise-only (Kafka/NATS/SQS/GCP/Azure are gated behind $enterpriseLicense in SidebarContent.svelte).

flows.title: Create an HTTP route from /routes that targets a deployed script
flows.story: As a developer with a deployed script, I open /routes from the sidebar, click 'New route', pick the script as the runnable, set a unique route path and an HTTP method (default POST), and Save — a row for the new route appears in the list and its canonical URL ('/api/r/<workspace>/<route_path>') invokes the script when called.

steps.action: From Home, navigate to /routes (sidebar TRIGGERS → 'HTTP'); confirm PageHeader title 'Custom HTTP routes'
Click the 'New route' button — the RouteEditor drawer opens
In the drawer, fill 'Summary' with a short label and leave the 'Path' field at its auto-generated 'u/<me>/route-<rand>'
Under 'Target' (Runnable selected by default), use the ScriptPicker to pick an existing deployed script
Under 'HTTP', fill the 'Path' (route_path) text input with a unique slug (e.g. 't01-<rand>') and leave http_method on 'POST'
Click 'Save' — the drawer closes
A row referencing the trigger path appears at /routes with the canonical URL containing POST and the route_path

scenarios.statement:

And I am on /routes
When I click 'New route'
And I pick <scriptPath> in the runnable ScriptPicker
And I fill the route_path 'Path' textbox with 't01-<rand>'

And POSTing to /api/r/<workspace_id>/t01-<rand> returns the script's result 'hello'

And route B is not persisted

flows.story: As a developer, I open the Triggers tab on a deployed script's detail page, copy the synchronous webhook URL from the Webhooks panel, POST a payload to it with a valid token, and see a corresponding row in /runs whose runnable matches the script_path.

steps.action: On /scripts/get/<scriptPath>, switch to the 'Triggers' tab in the right pane
Pick the default 'Webhooks' trigger — the WebhooksPanel renders under a 'Webhooks' section with the documentation link 'webhooks'
From WebhooksConfigSection, copy the 'sync' webhook URL: '<origin>/api/w/<workspace_id>/jobs/run_wait_result/p/<scriptPath>'
Issue a POST to that URL with an Authorization Bearer header carrying a workspace token and an empty JSON body
Response is the script's result; navigate to /runs and see a new row whose runnable matches <scriptPath>

Given I have a deployed script at <scriptPath> returning 'hello'
When I POST to '<origin>/api/w/<workspace_id>/jobs/run_wait_result/p/<scriptPath>' with a valid Bearer token and empty JSON body
Then the response body is 'hello'

features.name: Apps (low-code UI builder)
features.summary: Windmill Apps are low-code drag-drop UI dashboards. SCOPED here to the low-code app surface (NOT raw apps): builder at /apps/edit/<path> (AppEditor), in-workspace viewer at /apps/get/<path> (InWorkspaceAppViewer wrapping PublicAppFrame, rendering inside an opaque /app_embed/<wid>/<path> iframe for sandboxed apps), JSON definition surfaced at /apps/get_raw_app/<path>. Backend CRUD endpoints (windmill-api/src/apps.rs L89-130): POST /apps/create, POST /apps/update/<path>, DELETE /apps/delete/<path>, GET /apps/get/p/<path>, GET /apps/list. CreateApp shape (L316): { path, summary, value: AppValue (open JSON), policy: Policy { execution_mode: publisher | viewer | anonymous, ... } }. The builder UI is drag-drop with hundreds of selectors and is prohibitively brittle to drive via Playwright — tests use the API-precondition + UI-assertion split: POST /apps/create with a serialized AppValue (the minimal accepted shape is { grid: [], hiddenInlineScripts: [], css: {}, norefreshbar: false }), then drive only the runtime viewer / list UI for assertions. Apps are listed on the Home page under the 'Apps' toggle (kind=app#workspace) — there is no dedicated /apps list route.

Navigate to /apps/get/<path>; the InWorkspaceAppViewer mounts (its #app-edit-btn wrapper renders once getAppLiteByPath resolves writer permission for the seeded admin)

features.name: Raw apps (HTML/JS bundles)
features.summary: Raw apps are user-authored HTML/JS bundles (React/Svelte/Vue starter templates exist; runtime is framework-agnostic) deployed as the same `app` row as low-code apps but with the `app_version.raw_app` boolean flipped to true. The frontend authoring surface is /apps_raw/add and /apps_raw/edit/<path> (RawAppEditor — Monaco-backed multi-file IDE with hundreds of selectors, out-of-scope for authored UI flows for the same reason A* flows skip the low-code builder). The viewer at /apps_raw/get/<path> is a thin wrapper around the same InWorkspaceAppViewer the low-code /apps/get route uses; it sets the document <title> to `App <path>` via +page.js. Backend (windmill/backend/windmill-api/src/apps.rs L89-130): CRUD lives on the apps router with multipart bodies — POST /apps/create_raw, POST /apps/update_raw/<path>, both fields { app (json), js, css? } (the js bundle is required; create_app_internal stores it in app_bundles keyed by app_version_id). DELETE /apps/delete/<path> and GET /apps/get/p/<path> are SHARED with low-code apps; the response includes `raw_app: true` for raw rows. The `windmill-api/src/raw_apps.rs` file is a separate read-only legacy router (GET /raw_apps/list, /raw_apps/get_data/{version}/{path}) backed by a `raw_app` table that is empty on a CE install — new raw apps go to the `app` table via /apps/create_raw, so /raw_apps/list returns [] for them. Update gotcha: for raw-app updates the EditApp `value` field MUST be supplied — update_app_internal only INSERTs a new app_version row when ns.value is Some(_); without it the multipart handler tries to re-upload the JS bundle against the prior version id and trips the app_bundles_pkey unique constraint (HTTP 400 SqlErr). CE-supported (verified against the live local stack: POST /apps/create_raw → 201, POST /apps/update_raw → 200 when `value` is set, DELETE → 200).

The row is persisted in the `app` table with `raw_app: true`; GET /apps/get/p/<path> returns the round-tripped bundle bytes; navigating to /apps_raw/get/<path> mounts the InWorkspaceAppViewer (the same viewer the low-code /apps/get route uses) and the document <title> reflects the app path.

features.name: Workers and worker groups
features.summary: Workers are background processes that pull jobs from the queue and execute scripts/flows. The /workers route renders a read-mostly admin page (CenteredPage + PageHeader 'Workers') that polls WorkerService.listWorkers({ perPage: 1000, pingSince: 300 }) every 5s and ConfigService.listWorkerGroups() in parallel, grouping pings by (worker_instance, worker_group) and rendering one Tabs entry per worker group with a per-tab `<n> worker(s)` count appended via the Tab `extra` snippet. The selected tab renders a WorkerGroup card (group config tags, memory cap, action buttons Edit config / Delete config / Clean cache / Restart workers) followed by an 'Active workers' DataTable with columns Worker | Worker tags? | Worker start | Jobs ran | Last job | Occupancy rate (15s/5m/30m/ever) | Memory usage (Windmill) | Limits | Version | Status | Repl, plus a TextInput placeholder `Search workers in group '<group>'` that calls filterWorkerGroupByNames (matches on worker name | worker_instance | ip). On the sandbox stack the well-known groups are `default` (3 alive workers named `wk-default-*`, broad language tags) and `native` (8 alive workers named `wk-native-*`, db-runtime tags). Most of the page's create/edit affordances (Manage tags, Queue metrics, New agent worker, New group config, Repl per worker, AutoscalingEvents) are gated on `$superadmin || $devopsRole` AND parts (autoscaling, the YAML import of group configs) are explicitly EE-gated. Backend: WorkerService.listWorkers → GET /api/workers/list, ConfigService.listWorkerGroups → GET /api/configs/list_worker_groups, WorkerService.getCustomTags → GET /api/w/<wid>/workers/custom_tags. Worker rows reflect server-side state (last_ping freshness drives the Alive/Dead Badge: isWorkerAlive = last_ping < 60); the page does not allow creating workers from the UI (workers register themselves on startup), so the CE-supported authored flows here are READ-ONLY assertions against the existing live workers.

features.name: AI agents & assistants
features.summary: Windmill ships two distinct AI surfaces that share one configuration model: (a) the global 'Ask AI' chat panel — a right-side region with aria-label='AI chat' toggled by the sidebar 'Ask AI' MenuButton or the Cmd/Ctrl+L shortcut (svelte:window onkeydown listener in frontend/src/lib/components/copilot/chat/AIChat.svelte L110-118 calls aiChatManager.toggleOpen()); (b) 'AI Agent' flow modules — a FlowModuleValue::AIAgent { input_transforms, tools, tag, omit_output_from_conversation } variant declared in backend/windmill-types/src/flows.rs L966-973 that orchestrates a tools-calling LLM step inside a flow. Both surfaces are configured from the same workspace page: /workspace_settings?tab=ai mounts AISettings.svelte (frontend/src/lib/components/workspaceSettings/AISettings.svelte L36-613), which renders a SettingsPageHeader with title 'Windmill AI' + the canonical description, an 'AI Providers' SettingCard listing one Toggle per AI_PROVIDERS entry (frontend/src/lib/components/copilot/lib.ts L60-110: OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic (Recommended), Mistral, DeepSeek, Google AI, Groq, OpenRouter, Together AI, AWS Bedrock, Custom AI), plus cards for Default chat model, Metadata generation model, Code completion, Custom system prompts, and ModelTokenLimits. Saving wires each provider's resource_path through the admin-only POST /api/w/<wid>/edit_copilot_config (backend/windmill-api/src/workspaces.rs L89-167) into workspace_settings.ai_config. The chat panel mounts the AIChatDisplay region regardless of whether copilot is enabled — without a wired provider the panel renders a disabled hint linking back to /workspace_settings?tab=ai (AIChat.svelte L42-63), so the region's aria-label and the 'Chat' header / keyboard-hint footnote always render. All flows that actually invoke an LLM (sending a chat message, running an aiagent flow step) require a real provider key resource and are out of scope on this credential-less CE sandbox; CE-feasible flows here cover the chat panel mount, the workspace AI settings page render, and the persistence + flow-detail-page rendering of an aiagent flow module.

The panel mounts regardless of provider configuration; without a wired provider it shows a disabled hint instead of accepting a prompt, but the region's chrome ('Chat' header + 'to open or close this chat' keyboard-hint footnote) still renders — so the mount path is fully testable on CE without any LLM credentials.

features.name: Audit logs
features.summary: Every workspace mutation (create/update/delete of scripts, flows, variables, schedules, users, folders, etc.) and every job execution writes one row into the audit_log table inside the same transaction as the mutation. The /audit_logs SvelteKit route (frontend/src/routes/(root)/(logged)/audit_logs/+page.svelte) renders an h1 'Audit logs' (L92), an AuditLogsFilters chrome (username / operation / action_kind / resource / before / after / scope / perPage), an AuditLogsTimeline pane, and an AuditLogsTable with five fixed columns: ID | Timestamp | Username | Operation | Resource (AuditLogsTable.svelte L131-143). On Community Edition the backend handlers in windmill-audit/src/audit_oss.rs are no-ops — audit_log() returns Ok(()), list_audit() returns vec![], get_audit() returns 404 'Audit log not available in Windmill Community edition'. On the running sandbox stack the EE binary is shipped but NO EE license key is wired in, so the backend lets rows through but masks each one: operation='redacted', resource='EE only', parameters={redacted:'-'}; the page therefore renders the warning Alert 'You need an enterprise license to see unredacted audit logs.' (audit_logs/+page.svelte L154-158) above the table. The id / timestamp / username / workspace_id / action_kind columns survive redaction and are the only fields that this feature's CE-feasible tests assert. Backend route: GET /api/w/{workspace}/audit/list (windmill-api/src/audit.rs L21+34); ListAuditLogQuery accepts username, operation, action_kind, resource, before, after, all_workspaces (windmill-audit/src/lib.rs L30-41). The action_kind filter is bound to the Postgres ACTION_KIND enum which is declared #[sqlx(rename_all = "lowercase")] (lib.rs L9) — callers must pass 'create' / 'update' / 'delete' / 'execute'; capitalised values fall through to PG and surface as a SqlErr. The Serialize side has no rename, so the JSON response writes 'Create' / 'Update' / 'Delete' / 'Execute'. Out-of-CE-scope features explicitly marked distinct: S3 archive export (GET /settings/audit_logs_s3_status — openapi.yaml L1686), unredacted operation/resource/parameters columns, AuditLogsTimeline graph drill-down. Operators are blocked from /audit_logs unless workspace.operator_settings.audit_logs is true (audit_logs/+page.svelte L83-87).

features.name: Assets
features.summary: The /assets route is a workspace-wide, read-only catalog of every asset referenced by deployed scripts/flows in the workspace. Backend: GET /api/w/<wid>/assets/list (windmill-api-assets/src/lib.rs L18-356). The endpoint joins the `asset` table with `script`/`flow`/`v2_job` and excludes rows whose usage_path no longer resolves to a live runnable — so asset rows are not a denormalisation of the resource/variable tables, they are records of *usage*. Asset rows are inserted into `asset` by the deploy-time parser (windmill-common/src/assets.rs::insert_static_asset_usage + replace_static_asset_usage) when a script/flow's source references an asset via the SDK call patterns the parser recognises; the parser emits rows for kinds: s3object, resource, ducklake, datatable, volume. Variable references (wmill.get_variable(...) on the Python side) are NOT parsed into asset rows on CE on the sandbox stack — verified during walk (creating a variable and a script that calls wmill.get_variable does not surface a variable-kind asset). Frontend: routes/(root)/(logged)/assets/+page.svelte (the SvelteKit page) + +page.js (stuff.title='Assets' → document title 'Assets | Windmill'). The PageHeader renders an h1 'Assets' alongside a 'Pipelines' button linking to /pipeline. Two Sections: 'All workspace assets' shows three cards built from non-`asset`-table sources (Data table, Ducklake, Object storage — Volumes is folded into the Object storage card as the itemExtra Button snippet); 'Latest assets used' renders a DataTable (frontend/src/lib/components/table) whose columns are: icon (Tooltip showing formatAssetKind), Asset name (path + kind-label sub-line via formatAssetKind), <n> usage(s) link (opens AssetsUsageDrawer), Explore button (ExploreAssetButton when assetCanBeExplored). Empty state: a single row 'No assets found' (assets/+page.svelte L370). Filters: FilterSearchbar component (placeholder 'Filter assets...') backed by useUrlSyncedFilterInstance — every key the schema declares (asset_path, asset_kinds, usage_path, path, columns, _default_/broad_filter) is round-tripped through URL search params. Visiting /assets?asset_kinds=resource directly is equivalent to typing the tag in the filter chip. The 'All workspace assets' settings buttons (windmill_data_tables / ducklake / windmill_lfs) are admin-only via `$userStore?.is_admin || $superadmin` (assets/+page.svelte L203-213). Operator persona is blocked from the entire page unless workspace.operator_settings.assets=true (assets/+page.svelte L154-158). The S3FilePicker / AssetsUsageDrawer / VolumesDrawer mounts are read-only (`readOnlyMode allowDelete` for S3FilePicker, drawer-only viewer for the others) — there is no 'create asset' affordance because assets are a side-effect of script/flow deploys.

features.name: Concurrency groups
features.summary: Windmill concurrency groups bind a queueable resource (a script's `concurrent_limit` + `concurrency_key`, or a flow's identical knobs) to a row in the `concurrency_counter` table. When the number of in-flight jobs for that key exceeds `concurrent_limit` within `concurrency_time_window_s`, the worker dispatcher refuses to dequeue further jobs and they stay in v2_job_queue until an inflight job frees a slot. The /concurrency_groups SvelteKit route (frontend/src/routes/(root)/(logged)/concurrency_groups/+page.svelte L1-123) renders a CenteredPage + PageHeader 'Concurrency Groups' (.svelte L67) + a 'Refresh' Button, then polls ConcurrencyGroupsService.listConcurrencyGroups() every 2s. The TableCustom mounts ONLY when concurrencyGroups.length > 0 (L78), so the empty-state UI is the absence of the table (no 'No groups' copy is rendered). Each row has three columns: Concurrency key (an <a> to /runs/?concurrency_key=<key>) | Jobs running (total_running count from the live concurrency_counter join) | a per-row Trash button that calls ConcurrencyGroupsService.deleteConcurrencyGroup({ concurrencyId }). Backend (windmill-api-jobs/src/concurrency_groups.rs): GET /api/concurrency_groups/list reads `concurrency_counter` and returns one ConcurrencyGroups { concurrency_key, total_running } row per concurrency_id (L42-62; require_admin gate at L46). DELETE /api/concurrency_groups/prune/{*concurrency_key} (L64-108) deletes from `concurrency_counter` AND `concurrency_key` inside one transaction — but ONLY if total_running=0; otherwise it returns 500 'Concurrency group is currently in use, unable to remove it. Retry later.'. The /api/w/<wid>/concurrency_groups/list_jobs endpoint (workspaced; L141+) is consumed by the runs page when filtering by ?concurrency_key= and is out of scope here. Concurrency knobs are accepted on POST /scripts/create (NewScript schema in openapi.yaml L22772-22833: concurrent_limit, concurrency_time_window_s, concurrency_key) and POST /flows/create.

The page deliberately renders NO empty-state copy below the header — the TableCustom only mounts when concurrencyGroups.length > 0 — so the absence of a table is the empty state.

flows.story: As an admin or developer who wants to validate that the concurrency surface is wired end-to-end, I deploy a tiny Python script with concurrent_limit=1, concurrency_time_window_s=30, and a fresh concurrency_key, run it via the API, then confirm (a) /api/concurrency_groups/list contains a row keyed by my concurrency_key, and (b) the same key text is visible inside the TableCustom on /concurrency_groups. On EE the second concurrent run would queue behind the first; on CE both run immediately because update_concurrency_counter is a no-op (jobs_oss.rs L12-24), but the FIRST push under the key inserts the concurrency_counter row (jobs.rs L6170-6182 ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) — that row is what the page renders.

When I DELETE /api/concurrency_groups/prune/cg03_<slug>
Then the response is HTTP 500
And the response body contains 'Concurrency group is currently in use, unable to remove it. Retry later.'

Given I am signed in as a non-admin workspace user
When I DELETE /api/concurrency_groups/prune/<any-key>
Then the response is 4xx with 'Only administrators can delete concurrency groups'

features.name: Global drafts
features.summary: /global_drafts is a dev-only inspector for cross-kind in-progress drafts (scripts, flows, raw apps, schedules, triggers, resources, variables) in the current workspace. Two stacked sources feed it: (1) the per-user persisted drafts table — listGlobalDrafts() (frontend/src/lib/components/copilot/chat/global/userDraftAdapter.ts L492-508) calls DraftService.listDrafts({workspace}) which hits GET /api/w/<wid>/drafts/list (workspaced; drafts.rs L24-30, L82-141); (2) live in-tab UserDraft cells overlaid on top (same file L500-506). The page is hidden behind a localStorage feature gate (frontend/src/lib/components/copilot/chat/global/gate.ts L17-26): isGlobalAiEnabled() returns true iff localStorage.wm_dev_global_ai === '1'. Without that key the page's onMount goto('/')s, so every test MUST set the key in addInitScript before navigating. Frontend: routes/(root)/(logged)/global_drafts/+page.svelte L1-133 + +page.js L1-5 (stuff.title='Global AI drafts' → document.title 'Global AI drafts | Windmill'). The on-page h1 is the divergent 'Global local drafts' (.svelte L80). PageHeader exposes a single 'Clear all' Button (header, L83-90) which is ALWAYS in the DOM but `disabled={drafts.length === 0}`. The list itself is a <ul> of <li> rows: each renders `<kind>[(<triggerKind>)] · <path>` in a monospaced span (L101-108), an optional summary/language sub-line (L109-114), an iconOnly Trash Button with no accessible name (L116-122), and a JSON pre-dump of the value (L123-126). Empty state is the literal copy 'No drafts.' (L93-94). The auto-refresh is a setInterval(refreshDrafts, 1000) (L34) on top of the in-tab `storage` event listener — so a backend mutation lands in the DOM within ~1s without a reload, but reloads are deterministic. Backend (windmill/backend/windmill-api/src/drafts.rs): the workspaced router mounts /drafts/list, /drafts/get/{kind}/{*path}, /drafts/get_own/..., /drafts/update/{kind}/{*path}, /drafts/migrate_legacy/.... POST /drafts/update with `{value: <json>}` upserts and `{value: null}` deletes (own-discard bypasses the write-permission gate, L286-289). list_drafts (L82-141) returns DraftListItem rows including `mine`, `can_write`, `draft_only` (computed per-kind against the deployed table — script/flow/app/raw_app), and `draft_users` (only populated for the shared full-page-editor kinds). The kinds enum lives in windmill-common/src/user_drafts.rs::UserDraftItemKind; the GLOBAL_DRAFT_KINDS subset enumerated by the page is in userDraftAdapter.ts L38-54.

flows.title: View the /global_drafts page chrome (h1 + Clear all)
flows.story: As a Windmill developer running the dev-gated /global_drafts inspector, I open /global_drafts after setting localStorage.wm_dev_global_ai='1', see the 'Global local drafts' h1 and the 'Clear all' Button so I can confirm the inspector mounts whether or not any drafts exist in my workspace.

steps.action: Sign in as admin@windmill.dev
API precondition: GET /api/w/admins/drafts/list returns 200 + an array (drafts.rs L82-141; empty array is the valid empty-state response)
Open the page with localStorage.workspace='admins' AND localStorage.wm_dev_global_ai='1' set in addInitScript
Navigate to /global_drafts
Assert document.title contains 'Global AI drafts' (+page.js sets stuff.title='Global AI drafts')
Assert the h1 'Global local drafts' is visible (the heading text DIVERGES from the document title — see walkNotes)
Assert a Button labelled 'Clear all' is visible (always present in the DOM; only its `disabled` attribute changes with drafts.length)

scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in as 'admin@windmill.dev'
And the seeded 'admins' workspace is selected
And localStorage.wm_dev_global_ai is '1'
When I GET /api/w/admins/drafts/list
Then the response is 200 and the body is a JSON array
When I navigate to /global_drafts
Then the document title contains 'Global AI drafts'
And the h1 'Global local drafts' is visible
And a Button labelled 'Clear all' is visible

Given I am signed in as 'admin@windmill.dev'
And localStorage.wm_dev_global_ai is NOT set
When I navigate to /global_drafts
Then the page's onMount calls goto('/') and the URL becomes '/'

flows.title: Create a draft via the API → row appears on /global_drafts
flows.story: As a Windmill developer who wants to validate that the global drafts inspector reflects the persisted-draft source of truth, I POST a NewScript-shaped draft body to /api/w/admins/drafts/update/script/<path>, then open /global_drafts and see a row with my path and summary text.

steps.action: Sign in as admin@windmill.dev
Generate a fresh path 'u/admin/gd02-<slug>' (slug includes the test ns so parallel workers do not collide)
POST /api/w/admins/drafts/update/script/u/admin/gd02-<slug> with {value: {summary, content, language: 'python3', path, kind: 'script', ...}}
Assert the response is 200 with {status: 'saved'}
Sanity GET /api/w/admins/drafts/list: a row with kind='script' AND path='u/admin/gd02-<slug>' exists, draft_only=true, mine=true, can_write=true
Open /global_drafts with localStorage.wm_dev_global_ai='1'
Assert the h1 'Global local drafts' is visible
Assert a cell whose text contains 'u/admin/gd02-<slug>' is visible (per-row <li> renders the path verbatim)
Assert the row's summary sub-line 'gd02 draft <slug>' is visible
Cleanup: POST drafts/update with {value: null} to discard the draft

scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in as 'admin@windmill.dev'
And localStorage.wm_dev_global_ai is '1'
And a fresh slug '<slug>'
When I POST /api/w/admins/drafts/update/script/u/admin/gd02-<slug> with {value: <NewScript shape with summary 'gd02 draft <slug>'>}
Then the response is 200 with status 'saved'
And GET /api/w/admins/drafts/list contains a row {kind:'script', path:'u/admin/gd02-<slug>', draft_only:true, mine:true, can_write:true}
When I navigate to /global_drafts
Then the h1 'Global local drafts' is visible
And a cell whose text contains 'u/admin/gd02-<slug>' is visible
And the row summary 'gd02 draft <slug>' is visible

flows.title: Discard a draft → its row disappears from /global_drafts
flows.story: As a Windmill developer cleaning up an unwanted draft, I POST {value: null} to /api/w/admins/drafts/update/script/<path>, then reload /global_drafts and confirm the row I previously saw is gone — proving the inspector reflects the deleted state of the source of truth.

steps.action: Sign in as admin@windmill.dev
Generate a fresh path 'u/admin/gd03-<slug>'
POST /api/w/admins/drafts/update/script/u/admin/gd03-<slug> with a NewScript-shaped value (precondition: the row must exist)
Open /global_drafts (gated) and confirm a cell containing 'u/admin/gd03-<slug>' is visible
POST /api/w/admins/drafts/update/script/u/admin/gd03-<slug> with {value: null} — own-discard, skips the write-perm gate (drafts.rs L286-289)
Assert the response is 200 with {status: 'saved'}
Sanity GET /api/w/admins/drafts/list: no row with our path
Reload /global_drafts
Assert no cell whose text contains 'u/admin/gd03-<slug>' is visible (web-first toHaveCount(0))
Cleanup: idempotent delete (own-discard returns 'saved' even when the row is already gone, drafts.rs L383-391)

scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in as 'admin@windmill.dev'
And localStorage.wm_dev_global_ai is '1'
And a draft has been POSTed at 'u/admin/gd03-<slug>'
And /global_drafts shows a row containing 'u/admin/gd03-<slug>'
When I POST /api/w/admins/drafts/update/script/u/admin/gd03-<slug> with {value: null}
Then the response is 200 with status 'saved'
And GET /api/w/admins/drafts/list does NOT contain a row at 'u/admin/gd03-<slug>'
When I reload /global_drafts
Then no cell whose text contains 'u/admin/gd03-<slug>' is visible

Given a draft row is visible on /global_drafts
When I enumerate every <button> with an accessible name matching /Trash/
Then 0 matches are found (the per-row Button is rendered iconOnly, +page.svelte L116-122)
And the only labelled Button on the page is the header 'Clear all'
Therefore a UI-driven per-row delete MUST use a structural locator (e.g. `page.locator('ul li button').first()`) — not a role-based one

journeys.title: A new developer ships a scheduled Python script end-to-end
journeys.story: As a new developer, I sign in to Windmill, create a workspace, write a small Python script, run it from the auto-generated UI, schedule it on a cron, and verify the scheduled run appears in the runs history — so I can prove the platform supports the full author-to-schedule loop.

entry.summary: A fresh, signed-in developer with no workspace yet — lands on /user/workspaces.
entry.established: Auth is provisioned programmatically against the local Windmill backend (admin@windmill.dev / changeme; a Playwright storageState is reused so sign-in is not re-walked every run). The (logged) layout requires localStorage.workspace; without it every feature route redirects to /user/workspaces?rd=… — so workspace-create is the true first walked step.

journeys.title: An ops engineer composes a resource+variable-driven flow and fires it via an HTTP route
journeys.story: As an ops-minded developer, I add a Resource and a Variable, author a Python script that consumes both, compose a 2-step flow that calls that script, expose the flow via an HTTP route, fire the route, and inspect the resulting job — so I can prove the full configure → compose → trigger → inspect loop works end-to-end.

entry.summary: A signed-in admin in the seeded 'admins' workspace, with at least one deployed Python script already available in the workspace (preconditioned by the variables-and-resources / scripts feature walks).
entry.established: Auth is provisioned programmatically against the local Windmill backend (admin@windmill.dev / changeme) by helpers/userApi.loginAdmin; a Playwright storageState is reused across specs so sign-in is not re-walked. The (logged) layout requires localStorage.workspace = 'admins'; this is also seeded in the storageState so feature routes are reachable directly without going through /user/workspaces.

journeys.title: An admin stands up a team workspace, invites a teammate, organises them into a group + folder, and reviews activity in audit logs
journeys.story: As a workspace admin, I create a new team workspace, switch into it, invite a teammate via Workspace Settings, create a group on /groups and add the teammate as a member, create a folder on /folders, drop a small resource into f/<folder>/ to prove the folder ACL is wired, then navigate to /audit_logs and verify the prior mutations appear as rows — so I can prove the platform supports the full provision → permission → audit loop a team admin owns.

entry.summary: A signed-in admin in the seeded 'admins' workspace (admin@windmill.dev / changeme); reused Playwright storageState skips the sign-in walk. localStorage.workspace = 'admins' is seeded so feature routes are reachable without going through the picker; the journey's first walked step navigates to /user/workspaces to start a fresh team workspace.
entry.established: Auth is provisioned programmatically against the local Windmill backend by helpers/userApi.loginAdmin; a Playwright storageState is reused across specs so sign-in is not re-walked. Because the 'admins' workspace is EE-gated for non-admin invites on Community Edition (W05.S2), the journey MUST create a fresh team workspace first; invites/group-membership are then run in that fresh workspace.

journeys.title: A builder turns a script into a low-code app, publishes it, and shares it with a group
journeys.story: As a developer wearing the builder hat, I take an existing Python script, wrap it in a low-code app whose button invokes the script and surfaces the result, publish the app, then create a group, grant the group write access to the app, and confirm the published app still renders for the owner — so I can prove the platform supports the full script -> app -> share-with-group loop a builder owns.

entry.summary: A signed-in admin in the seeded 'admins' workspace via Playwright storageState; the journey is about TURNING an existing script into an app, so the script is deployed via API as a precondition rather than re-walked from scratch.
entry.established: Auth is provisioned programmatically against the local Windmill backend (admin@windmill.dev / changeme) by helpers/userApi.loginAdmin; storageState is reused across specs so sign-in is not re-walked. localStorage.workspace = 'admins' is seeded so feature routes are reachable without going through the picker.

journeys.title: A developer executes a script and confirms the pending run completes
journeys.story: As a developer, I open a script in my workspace, execute it, and confirm the pending run completes successfully in the runs history — so I can prove a script authored in the editor becomes an executable job whose result is observable end-to-end.

entry.summary: A signed-in developer already inside a workspace — lands on the scripts area with a workspace selected.
entry.established: Auth + workspace are provisioned programmatically against the local Windmill backend (admin@windmill.dev / changeme) and reused via a Playwright storageState so sign-in and workspace selection are not re-walked every run. workspaceId is supplied by the entry, not walked; the first walked step is opening/running a script.
